records.ety: [OE. & F. route, OF. rote, fr. L. rupta (sc. via), fr. ruptus, p. p. of rumpere to break; hence, literally, a broken or beaten way or path. See Rout, and cf. Rut a track.]

defs: The course or way which is traveled or passed, or is to be passed; a passing; a course; a road or path; a march.
quotes: Wide through the furzy field their route they take.
